title: Introductory Statistics for Scientists

prerequisites.text: A pass in any of MAM1004F/S or MAM1005H or MAM1031F or MAM1033F. Note: A student cannot obtain credits for more than one of STA1000F/S/P/L, STA1007S, STA1006S, STA1008F/S. This course is the equivalent of STA1000S in a biological setting.

outline: This course aims to provide an introduction to statistics for Science students. Topics covered include: exploratory data analysis and summary statistics; set theory; probability (conditional probabilities, independence, Bayes theorem); random variables; probability mass and density functions; Binomial, Poisson, Exponential, Normal and Uniform distributions; sampling distributions; confidence intervals; hypothesis testing (Z-test and t-test for means and difference between means, Chi-square test for independence and goodness-of-fit); meaning of p-values; determining sample size; simple linear regression and measures of correlation. Practical data analysis is taught using R.
lecture_times: Five lectures per week, Monday - Friday, 1st period.
dp_requirements: Satisfactory attendance of lectures, tutorials, practicals and tests and completion of assignments and/or class exercises as set out in course outline. Class record of at least 35%.
assessment: Class record 40% and a 3-hour exam counting 60%. Weights will be adjusted in the case of missed assessments, as detailed in the course outline.
